title: 19 men charged in mass rapes case among 'libertines' in France
subhead: A man identified as Christophe B. is accused of having forced six former partners into extremely violent sexual encounters with dozens of men recruited online over a period of more than 20 years, under the guise of 'libertinism.'

French regulators order block of Polymarket's website
subhead: The French gambling regulator said the site's continued availability constituted advertising. A ban on financial transactions to the site was already in place.

title: Trump delivers conspiracy-tinged speech on US elections and accuses China of interference
subhead: In a televised address on Thursday, the US president contested, once again, the 2020 US presidential election results, referencing a so-called 'deep state' that allegedly hid evidence of Chinese meddling.

title: Behind the scenes of Macron's last G7, as told by photographer Kamil Zihnioglu
subhead: There is protocol, and then there is Donald Trump. That, in essence, is what photographer Kamil Zihnioglu took away from his three days covering the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains for Le Monde.

title: The troubling Russian connections of a 'European' password manager
subhead: Although it presents itself as a Spanish company, Passwork was actually developed in Russia and operates with a license from Russia's FSB domestic intelligence agency. The situation raises major security questions for its customers, including several French companies.

'It's Mad Max on the ocean': The cocaine-loaded speedboats racing to Europe's shores
subhead: In the Atlantic, hundreds of powerful go-fast boats, custom-built to transport tons of cocaine, retrieve shipments from vessels traveling commercial routes between South America, Africa and Europe, according to an investigation by Le Monde in partnership with NDR, WDR, Süddeutsche Zeitung, NRC, IrpiMedia and The Washington Post.
byline: Liselotte Mas,  Elsa Delmas and  Thomas Saintourens

title: Morocco's frenzy for purchasing surveillance tools revealed by whistleblower
subhead: Keyloggers installed via USB stick, phones sold with pre-installed spyware, wiretapping and hidden cameras... In addition to the Pegasus spyware, Morocco has assembled a powerful arsenal of repressive tools that it deploys against journalists and political targets.

title: How the 2016 Nice terrorist attack unfolded, minute-by-minute
subhead: On July 14, 2016, a terrorist drove a truck into crowds gathered on the famed Promenade des Anglais in the southern French city, killing 86 people. Ten years later, Le Monde looks back at how the tragedy unfolded.
